- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.

## Task #2. SQL Query

You have two tables:

- `user` table: `user_id`, `installed_at` (app installation date)
- `payment` table: `user_id`, `payment_at` (payment date), `amount` (payment amount)

Write an SQL query that calculates the cumulative ARPU (Average Revenue Per User), grouped by payment month, considering only users who installed the app in January 2023.

---

## 🤖 Task #3. LLM-driven dashboard on games.csv

This is the core task. We want to see how you use an LLM (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, ChatGPT, any tool you prefer) to build a production-grade interactive dashboard.

### Dataset

`games.csv`, video game industry history. [Link](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QXvqgwjo_PJixFRcapKhjyca_Cd-58pr/view) to the dataset.

Fields:

- Name
- Platform
- Year_of_Release
- Genre
- Critic_Score
- User_Score
- Rating (age rating: E, E10, T, M)

Preprocessing (required): drop rows released before 1990 or after 2010, and drop any rows with missing values in any column.

[`games.csv`](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1QXvqgwjo_PJixFRcapKhjyca_Cd-58pr/view) is attached to this assignment (ask the recruiter if you did not receive it).

### What to build

An interactive dashboard, deployed to a public URL. **Two tabs on one page.**

**Tab 1, "Dashboard"**, the dashboard itself. Minimum requirements:

- At least 3 filters: platform (multi-select), genre (multi-select), year range
- At least 3 KPI numbers (for example: total games, average critic score, average user score, or your own choice)
- At least 3 interactive charts that all react to filter changes at once
- Responsive (works on desktop and mobile)
- Modern visual style. We attached reference screenshots below

![Reference visual, design style only. Data on the reference dashboard is illustrative.](https://middle-da-test-reference.vercel.app/reference-light.png)

Reference visual, design style only. Data on the reference dashboard is illustrative.

- The exact chart set is up to you. We want to see product thinking, not a pixel-copy of a layout

**Tab 2, "LLM Session"**, a verbatim machine-readable export of how you worked with the LLM: prompts, replies, iterations, hallucinations you caught, fixes, and verification steps.

Any of these formats is fine:

- `.jsonl` from Claude Code
- Cursor chat export
- Public link to a ChatGPT/Claude.ai shared chat
- Structured markdown (prompt → reply → fix)

The point is that an analyzer can read it. Not "general description of my approach", but the real log with prompts.

Include your verification steps inside the log: SQL checks, sanity checks, screenshots, manual recomputations, anything you used to confirm the LLM did not lie. We use these to evaluate how you caught hallucinations.

Optional: a "How I built this" section, 5–10 sentences on the structure of your prompts, how you caught hallucinations, what you edited by hand.

### Stack and deployment

Pick the stack you prefer. Suggestion: vanilla HTML/CSS/JS + Chart.js, or React/Vue + Recharts. The result must look modern.

Deploy anywhere public (Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare Pages, GitHub Pages, Render, Railway). The URL must open without login.

### What we evaluate (Task 3)

- Prompt quality: context, constraints, iterations. Not one-liners
- Whether you caught LLM hallucinations, and how
- Final product: design, UX, correctness, responsiveness

## Overall scoring

Tasks 1 and 2 are reviewed by the hiring manager (SQL, attention to detail).

Task 3 is reviewed automatically (we run your session export through an analyzer) and visually (we look at the live dashboard).

<aside>
🕐 **Deadline: 3 days from the moment you receive the assignment.**

</aside>

## Deliverables

1. SQL files for tasks 1 and 2 (`.sql`)
2. Public link to the deployed two-tab dashboard
3. Optional: link to a public GitHub/GitLab repo with sources

input.content:
# Games Dashboard — Implementation Plan

## Context
This is a take-home assignment with 3 deliverables:
1. SQL file for Task 1 (not detailed in brief — placeholder)
2. SQL file for Task 2 (cumulative ARPU by payment month, Jan 2023 cohort)
3. A deployed two-tab interactive dashboard built on `games.csv`

Stack: React + Recharts, deployed to Vercel.

---

## Phase 1 — Data Preprocessing (Node.js script)

**File:** `scripts/preprocess.js`

Read `games.csv`, apply required filters:
- Drop rows where `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `Year_of_Release > 2010`
- Drop rows with any missing/null/empty values in any column

Output: `public/games_clean.json` — the clean dataset bundled with the app (no backend needed, static deployment).

Also extract unique values for filter dropdowns: `platforms[]`, `genres[]`, `years[]`.

---

## Phase 2 — React App Scaffold

**Bootstrap:** `npx create-react-app games-dashboard` (or Vite + React for speed)

Preferred: **Vite + React** — faster build, smaller bundle, simpler config.

```
games-dashboard/
  public/
    games_clean.json
  src/
    App.jsx              # tab routing
    components/
      FilterPanel.jsx    # platform multi-select, genre multi-select, year range slider
      KpiCards.jsx       # 3 KPI number cards
      charts/
        GenreBarChart.jsx
        PlatformPieChart.jsx
        YearTrendLine.jsx
      LLMSession.jsx     # Tab 2 — renders the session log
    hooks/
      useGamesData.js    # load + filter logic
    styles/
      index.css
```

---

## Phase 3 — Tab 1: Dashboard

### Filters (FilterPanel)
- Platform: multi-select checkboxes or react-select
- Genre: multi-select
- Year range: dual-handle range slider (react-range or native input[type=range] ×2)

All filters applied simultaneously; filtered dataset passed down to all charts and KPIs.

### KPI Cards (KpiCards)
1. **Total Games** — count of filtered rows
2. **Avg Critic Score** — mean of `Critic_Score`
3. **Avg User Score** — mean of `User_Score`

### Charts (all react to filters)
1. **Games by Genre** — horizontal bar chart (Recharts `BarChart`)
2. **Platform Distribution** — pie/donut chart (Recharts `PieChart`)
3. **Releases Over Time** — line chart by year (Recharts `LineChart`)

### Style
- Dark or clean light theme, modern card layout
- CSS Grid for responsive layout (2-col desktop → 1-col mobile)
- Color palette: slate/indigo or similar

---

## Phase 4 — Tab 2: LLM Session

A rendered markdown view of this very Claude Code conversation — exported as a structured `.md` or `.jsonl` from the Claude Code session.

The `LLMSession.jsx` component will render the log inline (import as a module or fetch from `/llm-session.md`).

---

## Phase 5 — SQL Files

**`task2.sql`** — Cumulative ARPU:

```sql
WITH jan_users AS (
  SELECT user_id
  FROM user
  WHERE DATE_TRUNC('month', installed_at) = '2023-01-01'
),
monthly_revenue AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', p.payment_at) AS payment_month,
    SUM(p.amount) AS revenue
  FROM payment p
  JOIN jan_users u ON p.user_id = u.user_id
  GROUP BY 1
),
cumulative AS (
  SELECT
    payment_month,
    revenue,
    SUM(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY payment_month) AS cumulative_revenue
  FROM monthly_revenue
)
SELECT
  payment_month,
  revenue,
  cumulative_revenue,
  cumulative_revenue / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM jan_users) AS cumulative_arpu
FROM cumulative
ORDER BY payment_month;
```

---

## Phase 6 — Deployment

1. `npm run build` → `dist/`
2. `vercel --prod` from project root (or connect GitHub repo to Vercel dashboard)
3. Confirm public URL works

---

## Verification Steps

- Run preprocess script, confirm row count makes sense (no 1989 or 2011+ rows, no nulls)
- Check filter changes update all 3 charts simultaneously
- Test on mobile viewport (375px)
- Spot-check KPI math manually against raw CSV
- Confirm Vercel URL is publicly accessible without login

---

## File Order of Operations

1. `scripts/preprocess.js` → `public/games_clean.json`
2. Vite scaffold → install recharts, react-select, react-range
3. `useGamesData.js` hook (load + filter)
4. `FilterPanel.jsx` + `KpiCards.jsx`
5. Three chart components
6. `App.jsx` tab layout + responsive CSS
7. `LLMSession.jsx`
8. `task2.sql`
9. Deploy to Vercel

# Games Dashboard — Implementation Plan (Task 3 only)

## Context
Build a production-grade two-tab interactive dashboard on `games.csv`, deployed to a public Vercel URL.
Stack: React + Recharts + Vite.

---

## Phase 1 — Data Preprocessing

**File:** `scripts/preprocess.js` (run once, output bundled with app)

- Read `games.csv`
- Drop rows: `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `Year_of_Release > 2010`
- Drop rows with any missing value in any column
- Output: `public/games_clean.json`

No backend needed — static JSON loaded at runtime.

---

## Phase 2 — Project Scaffold

```
games-dashboard/         (Vite + React)
  public/
    games_clean.json
  src/
    App.jsx              # two-tab layout

# Games Dashboard — Implementation Plan

## Context
This is a take-home assignment with 3 deliverables:
1. SQL file for Task 1 (not detailed in brief — placeholder)
2. SQL file for Task 2 (cumulative ARPU by payment month, Jan 2023 cohort)
3. A deployed two-tab interactive dashboard built on `games.csv`

Stack: React + Recharts, deployed to Vercel.

---

## Phase 1 — Data Preprocessing (Node.js script)

**File:** `scripts/preprocess.js`

Read `games.csv`, apply required filters:
- Drop rows where `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `Year_of_Release > 2010`
- Drop rows with any missing/null/empty values in any column

Output: `public/games_clean.json` — the clean dataset bundled with the app (no backend needed, static deployment).

Also extract unique values for filter dropdowns: `platforms[]`, `genres[]`, `years[]`.

---

## Phase 2 — React App Scaffold

**Bootstrap:** `npx create-react-app games-dashboard` (or Vite + React for speed)

Preferred: **Vite + React** — faster build, smaller bundle, simpler config.

```
games-dashboard/
  public/
    games_clean.json
  src/
    App.jsx              # tab routing
    components/
      FilterPanel.jsx    # platform multi-select, genre multi-select, year range slider
      KpiCards.jsx       # 3 KPI number cards
      charts/
        GenreBarChart.jsx
        PlatformPieChart.jsx
        YearTrendLine.jsx
      LLMSession.jsx     # Tab 2 — renders the session log
    hooks/
      useGamesData.js    # load + filter logic
    styles/
      index.css
```

---

## Phase 3 — Tab 1: Dashboard

### Filters (FilterPanel)
- Platform: multi-select checkboxes or react-select
- Genre: multi-select
- Year range: dual-handle range slider (react-range or native input[type=range] ×2)

All filters applied simultaneously; filtered dataset passed down to all charts and KPIs.

### KPI Cards (KpiCards)
1. **Total Games** — count of filtered rows
2. **Avg Critic Score** — mean of `Critic_Score`
3. **Avg User Score** — mean of `User_Score`

### Charts (all react to filters)
1. **Games by Genre** — horizontal bar chart (Recharts `BarChart`)
2. **Platform Distribution** — pie/donut chart (Recharts `PieChart`)
3. **Releases Over Time** — line chart by year (Recharts `LineChart`)

### Style
- Dark or clean light theme, modern card layout
- CSS Grid for responsive layout (2-col desktop → 1-col mobile)
- Color palette: slate/indigo or similar

---

## Phase 4 — Tab 2: LLM Session

A rendered markdown view of this very Claude Code conversation — exported as a structured `.md` or `.jsonl` from the Claude Code session.

The `LLMSession.jsx` component will render the log inline (import as a module or fetch from `/llm-session.md`).

---

## Phase 5 — SQL Files

**`task2.sql`** — Cumulative ARPU:

```sql
WITH jan_users AS (
  SELECT user_id
  FROM user
  WHERE DATE_TRUNC('month', installed_at) = '2023-01-01'
),
monthly_revenue AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', p.payment_at) AS payment_month,
    SUM(p.amount) AS revenue
  FROM payment p
  JOIN jan_users u ON p.user_id = u.user_id
  GROUP BY 1
),
cumulative AS (
  SELECT
    payment_month,
    revenue,
    SUM(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY payment_month) AS cumulative_revenue
  FROM monthly_revenue
)
SELECT
  payment_month,
  revenue,
  cumulative_revenue,
  cumulative_revenue / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM jan_users) AS cumulative_arpu
FROM cumulative
ORDER BY payment_month;
```

---

## Phase 6 — Deployment

1. `npm run build` → `dist/`
2. `vercel --prod` from project root (or connect GitHub repo to Vercel dashboard)
3. Confirm public URL works

---

## Verification Steps

- Run preprocess script, confirm row count makes sense (no 1989 or 2011+ rows, no nulls)
- Check filter changes update all 3 charts simultaneously
- Test on mobile viewport (375px)
- Spot-check KPI math manually against raw CSV
- Confirm Vercel URL is publicly accessible without login

---

## File Order of Operations

1. `scripts/preprocess.js` → `public/games_clean.json`
2. Vite scaffold → install recharts, react-select, react-range
3. `useGamesData.js` hook (load + filter)
4. `FilterPanel.jsx` + `KpiCards.jsx`
5. Three chart components
6. `App.jsx` tab layout + responsive CSS
7. `LLMSession.jsx`
8. `task2.sql`
9. Deploy to Vercel

# Games Dashboard — Style Guide

Extracted from reference screenshot.

---

## Layout

- **Page background:** White `#FFFFFF`
- **Max content width:** ~1200px, centered
- **Spacing unit:** 8px base (padding/gap multiples of 8)
- **Border radius (cards):** 8–10px
- **Card style:** White background, subtle `1px solid #E5E7EB` border, no heavy shadow

### Grid Structure (top → bottom)
1. **Top nav bar** — full width, sticky
2. **Filter row** — 3 equal columns (Platform | Genre | Year Range)
3. **KPI row** — 3 equal columns
4. **Chart row 1** — 2 columns (~55% / 45% split)
5. **Chart row 2** — 1 full-width column

---

## Color Palette

| Role | Value |
|---|---|
| Page background | `#FFFFFF` |
| Card background | `#FFFFFF` |
| Card border | `#E5E7EB` |
| Nav border | `#F3F4F6` |
| Label text (uppercase headings) | `#9CA3AF` (gray-400) |
| Primary text | `#111827` (gray-900) |
| Secondary text | `#6B7280` (gray-500) |
| Accent / logo / slider | `#22C55E` (green-500) |
| Filter pill background | `#F3F4F6` (gray-100) |
| Filter pill text | `#374151` (gray-700) |
| Scatter / chart multi-color | see Chart Colors below |

### Chart Colors (multi-series palette)
```
Racing      #86EFAC  (green-300)
Sports      #60A5FA  (blue-400)
Platform    #FB923C  (orange-400)
Misc        #F472B6  (pink-400)
Shooter     #E879F9  (fuchsia-400)
Fighting    #38BDF8  (sky-400)
Action      #FBBF24  (amber-400)
Role-Playing #F87171 (red-400)
Adventure   #4ADE80  (green-400)
Simulation  #A78BFA  (violet-400)
MMO         #6D28D9  (violet-700)
Strategy    #065F46  (emerald-900)
```

---

## Typography

- **Font family:** System sans-serif stack — `Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif`
- **All-caps section labels:** `0.65rem`, `font-weight: 600`, `letter-spacing: 0.08em`, color `#9CA3AF`

| Element | Size | Weight | Color |
|---|---|---|---|
| Logo / app name | 14px | 600 | `#111827` |
| Tab labels | 13px | 500 | `#374151` |
| Page title (H1) | 26px | 700 | `#111827` |
| Page subtitle | 13px | 400 | `#6B7280` |
| Filter section header | 10px uppercase | 600 | `#9CA3AF` |
| Filter pill text | 12px | 500 | `#374151` |
| KPI label | 10px uppercase | 600 | `#9CA3AF` |
| KPI value | 36px | 700 | `#111827` |
| KPI sub-label | 12px | 400 | `#9CA3AF` |
| Chart title | 11px uppercase | 600 | `#9CA3AF` |
| Chart annotation (right-aligned) | 11px | 400 | `#9CA3AF` |
| Axis labels | 11px | 400 | `#9CA3AF` |
| Reset link | 12px | 500 | `#9CA3AF` |

---

## Components

### Top Navigation Bar
- Height: ~52px
- Background: `#FFFFFF`
- Bottom border: `1px solid #F3F4F6`
- Logo: Green square icon (rounded 4px) + "Games Dashboard" text
- Tabs: Pill-style toggle group, active tab has `1px solid #E5E7EB` border, rounded 6px
- Right side: "games.csv" download button (outlined, small) + dark mode toggle icon

### Filter Pills (Platform & Genre)
- Shape: `border-radius: 20px` (fully rounded)
- Background: `#F3F4F6`
- Padding: `4px 12px`
- Font: 12px, weight 500
- Gap between pills: 6px
- Selected state: not clearly distinguished in reference — use green border `1px solid #22C55E` or slightly darker bg
- "Reset" link: top-right corner of filter card, 12px gray text

### Year Range Slider
- Two handles (min year, max year)
- Track fill: `#22C55E`
- Track unfilled: `#E5E7EB`
- Handle: circle, `#22C55E` fill, white border
- Labels at ends: "1990" and "2010"

### KPI Cards
- Equal-width, horizontal row, full-width spanning
- No shadow — only border `1px solid #E5E7EB`
- Internal padding: 20px
- Value is large and bold, label is small uppercase above, sub-label below in gray

### Charts

**General:**
- No chart background fill (transparent on white card)
- Minimal grid lines — only horizontal, light `#F3F4F6`
- No vertical grid lines
- Axis labels: gray, small
- Legend: inline dot + label, 11px

**Horizontal Bar Chart (Genre)**
- Bar color: `#22C55E` (single color, green)
- Bars fully rounded on the right end (`border-radius` on bar right side)
- Category labels left-aligned on y-axis
- X-axis minimal, no title

**Scatter Plot (Critic vs User Score)**
- Dot size: ~6px radius
- Color by genre (multi-color palette above)
- Both axes labeled ("Critic score", "User score")
- Legend: two rows, top-right
- Dot opacity: ~0.75 to handle overlap

**Stacked Area Chart (Releases per Year)**
- Smooth curves (`curveMonotone` or `curveBasis`)
- Each platform a distinct color (use chart palette)
- Stacked (not overlapping)
- X-axis: years as labels, no rotation
- Legend: horizontal, centered above chart
- Top-right annotation: "stacked" label in gray

---

## Responsive Behavior

- **Desktop (≥1024px):** All multi-column layouts as described
- **Tablet (768–1023px):** Filter row → 2 cols + 1 below; KPI row → 2+1; Charts → 1 col each
- **Mobile (<768px):** All sections single column; filters scroll horizontally

---

## Dark Mode

Toggle visible in top-right nav (moon icon). Not specified in reference — implement as:
- Background: `#0F172A`
- Card: `#1E293B`, border `#334155`
- Text: `#F1F5F9`
- Labels: `#64748B`

- Nav bar ~52px, white, bottom border `#F3F4F6`
- Cards: `border-radius: 8px`, no shadow, border only
- Spacing: 8px base unit; card padding ~20px; gap between cards 16px
- Grid: 3-col filters → 3-col KPIs → 2-col charts → 1-col wide chart

## Key Component Notes
- Filter pills: fully rounded (`border-radius: 20px`), `#F3F4F6` bg
- Year slider: two handles, green track `#22C55E`
- Bar chart: single green color, right-end rounded bars
- Scatter: dots colored by category, ~0.75 opacity
- Area chart: stacked, smooth curves, multi-color by series

## Multi-series Color Palette (charts)
```
#60A5FA  #FB923C  #F472B6  #38BDF8  #FBBF24
#4ADE80  #A78BFA  #F87171  #E879F9  #86EFAC
```

content.text: Style guide saved to [STYLE_GUIDE.md](STYLE_GUIDE.md). It covers palette, typography, layout structure, key component patterns, and the multi-color chart palette — kept concise enough to reference quickly as we build other elements.

file.content:
# Games Dashboard — Implementation Plan (Task 3 only)

## Context
Build a production-grade two-tab interactive dashboard on `games.csv`, deployed to a public Vercel URL.
Stack: React + Recharts + Vite.

---

## Phase 1 — Data Preprocessing

**File:** `scripts/preprocess.js` (run once, output bundled with app)

- Read `games.csv`
- Drop rows: `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `Year_of_Release > 2010`
- Drop rows with any missing value in any column
- Output: `public/games_clean.json`

No backend needed — static JSON loaded at runtime.

---

## Phase 2 — Project Scaffold

```
games-dashboard/         (Vite + React)
  public/
    games_clean.json
  src/
    App.jsx              # two-tab layout
    components/
      FilterPanel.jsx    # platform multi-select, genre multi-select, year range
      KpiCards.jsx       # 3 KPI numbers
      charts/
        GenreBarChart.jsx
        PlatformPieChart.jsx
        YearTrendLine.jsx
      LLMSession.jsx     # Tab 2 — session log
    hooks/
      useFilteredGames.js  # load + filter logic
    styles/
      index.css
```

**Dependencies:** `recharts`, `react-select` (multi-select dropdowns)

---

## Phase 3 — Tab 1: Dashboard

### Filters (all applied simultaneously)
- **Platform** — multi-select (react-select)
- **Genre** — multi-select (react-select)
- **Year range** — two `<input type="range">` sliders for min/max year

### KPI Cards
1. Total Games (count)
2. Avg Critic Score
3. Avg User Score

### Charts (all react to filter changes)
1. **Games by Genre** — horizontal `BarChart` (Recharts)
2. **Platform Distribution** — `PieChart` / donut (Recharts)
3. **Releases Over Time** — `LineChart` by year (Recharts)

### Style
- Clean modern card layout, slate/indigo color palette
- CSS Grid: 2-col desktop → 1-col mobile (responsive)

---

## Phase 4 — Tab 2: LLM Session

- Export this Claude Code session as structured markdown (`llm-session.md`)
- Place in `public/llm-session.md`
- `LLMSession.jsx` fetches and renders it (using a markdown renderer like `react-markdown`)

---

## Phase 5 — Deployment

1. `npm run build` → `dist/`
2. Deploy to Vercel (`vercel --prod` or GitHub → Vercel dashboard)
3. Confirm public URL opens without login

---

## Verification Steps

- Run preprocess script; log row count before/after to confirm filters applied correctly
- Spot-check 2-3 KPI values manually against raw CSV
- Verify all 3 charts update when filters change
- Test responsive layout at 375px mobile width
- Confirm Vercel URL is publicly accessible

content.thinking:
Good. I can see:
1. The CSV has columns: `Name`, `Platform`, `Year_of_Release`, `Genre`, `Critic_Score`, `User_Score`, `Rating`
2. There are `nan` values in `Critic_Score` and empty strings in `User_Score` and `Rating`
3. Some records are from before 1990 (e.g., NES 1985, GB 1989)

So the verification script should check:
- Missing/null values per column
- Year range validity (1990-2010)
- Data types (numeric for scores, year)


- Value ranges (Critic_Score 0-100, User_Score 0-10)
- Valid Rating values (E, E10, T, M)
- Duplicate detection
- After preprocessing: confirm row counts, no nulls, year bounds respected

Now I'm laying out the core verification rules: schema validation to ensure all columns exist, null detection across all fields including malformed entries, year boundary checks for the 1990-2010 range, numeric validation for scores with their proper ranges, and rating category validation.

The workflow will load the raw CSV, run the full suite of checks and generate a report, then apply preprocessing steps like removing nulls and filtering years, run the checks again to confirm the data is clean, and finally export to JSON.

input.new_string:
## Phase 1 — Data Quality Verification (Python/pandas)

**File:** `scripts/verify_data.py`  
Run once before building; outputs a compact text report — Claude reads the report, not the raw CSV.

### Verification Rules

**A. Schema**
- All 7 columns present: `Name`, `Platform`, `Year_of_Release`, `Genre`, `Critic_Score`, `User_Score`, `Rating`

**B. Null / missing detection**
- Null count per column (includes actual `NaN`, string `"nan"`, empty string `""`)
- Flag any column with missing > 0

**C. Year range**
- Count rows where `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `> 2010`
- Show min/max year in dataset

**D. Score ranges**
- `Critic_Score`: must be numeric, 0–100
- `User_Score`: must be numeric, 0–10
- Report non-numeric values and out-of-range counts separately

**E. Rating validity**
- Allowed values: `E`, `E10+`, `T`, `M`, `AO`, `K-A`, `RP`
- Report any unexpected values

**F. Duplicates**
- Exact duplicate rows
- Duplicate `(Name, Platform)` pairs (same game on same platform)

**G. Post-preprocessing summary**
- Apply the two required filters (year range + drop any-null rows)
- Print: rows before → rows removed → rows after
- Confirm: 0 nulls remain, all years in [1990, 2010]
- Print unique counts: platforms, genres, years

### Output
Script prints a structured report to stdout (easily pasteable into LLM session log).

---

## Phase 2 — Data Preprocessing

**File:** `scripts/preprocess.py` (or merged into verify script as `--export` flag)

- Read `games.csv`
- Normalize: replace string `"nan"` and `""` with actual `NaN`
- Drop rows: `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `> 2010`
- Drop rows with any null in any column
- Convert `User_Score` to float, `Critic_Score` to int
- Output: `public/games_clean.json`

No backend needed — static JSON loaded at runtime.

# Games Dashboard — Implementation Plan (Task 3 only)

## Context
Build a production-grade two-tab interactive dashboard on `games.csv`, deployed to a public Vercel URL.
Stack: React + Recharts + Vite.

---

## Phase 1 — Data Quality Verification (Python/pandas)

**File:** `scripts/verify_data.py`  
Run once before building; outputs a compact text report — Claude reads the report, not the raw CSV.

### Verification Rules

**A. Schema**
- All 7 columns present: `Name`, `Platform`, `Year_of_Release`, `Genre`, `Critic_Score`, `User_Score`, `Rating`

**B. Null / missing detection**
- Null count per column (includes actual `NaN`, string `"nan"`, empty string `""`)
- Flag any column with missing > 0

**C. Year range**
- Count rows where `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `> 2010`
- Show min/max year in dataset

**D. Score ranges**
- `Critic_Score`: must be numeric, 0–100
- `User_Score`: must be numeric, 0–10
- Report non-numeric values and out-of-range counts separately

**E. Rating validity**
- Allowed values: `E`, `E10+`, `T`, `M`, `AO`, `K-A`, `RP`
- Report any unexpected values

**F. Duplicates**
- Exact duplicate rows
- Duplicate `(Name, Platform)` pairs (same game on same platform)

**G. Post-preprocessing summary**
- Apply the two required filters (year range + drop any-null rows)
- Print: rows before → rows removed → rows after
- Confirm: 0 nulls remain, all years in [1990, 2010]
- Print unique counts: platforms, genres, years

### Output
Script prints a structured report to stdout (easily pasteable into LLM session log).

---

## Phase 2 — Data Preprocessing

**File:** `scripts/preprocess.py` (or merged into verify script as `--export` flag)

- Read `games.csv`
- Normalize: replace string `"nan"` and `""` with actual `NaN`
- Drop rows: `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `> 2010`
- Drop rows with any null in any column
- Convert `User_Score` to float, `Critic_Score` to int
- Output: `public/games_clean.json`

No backend needed — static JSON loaded at runtime.

---

        PlatformPieChart.jsx
        YearTrendLine.jsx
      LLMSession.jsx     # Tab 2 — session log
    hooks/
      useFilteredGames.js  # load + filter logic
    styles/
      index.css
```

# Games Dashboard — Implementation Plan (Task 3 only)

## Context
Build a production-grade two-tab interactive dashboard on `games.csv`, deployed to a public Vercel URL.
Stack: React + Recharts + Vite.

---

## Phase 1 — Data Quality Verification (Python/pandas)

**File:** `scripts/verify_data.py`  
Run once before building; outputs a compact text report — Claude reads the report, not the raw CSV.

### Verification Rules

**A. Schema**
- All 7 columns present: `Name`, `Platform`, `Year_of_Release`, `Genre`, `Critic_Score`, `User_Score`, `Rating`

**B. Null / missing detection**
- Null count per column (includes actual `NaN`, string `"nan"`, empty string `""`)
- Flag any column with missing > 0

**C. Year range**
- Count rows where `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `> 2010`
- Show min/max year in dataset

**D. Score ranges**
- `Critic_Score`: must be numeric, 0–100
- `User_Score`: must be numeric, 0–10
- Report non-numeric values and out-of-range counts separately

**E. Rating validity**
- Allowed values: `E`, `E10+`, `T`, `M`, `AO`, `K-A`, `RP`
- Report any unexpected values

**F. Duplicates**
- Exact duplicate rows
- Duplicate `(Name, Platform)` pairs (same game on same platform)

**G. Post-preprocessing summary**
- Apply the two required filters (year range + drop any-null rows)
- Print: rows before → rows removed → rows after
- Confirm: 0 nulls remain, all years in [1990, 2010]
- Print unique counts: platforms, genres, years

### Output
Script prints a structured report to stdout (easily pasteable into LLM session log).

---

## Phase 2 — Data Preprocessing

**File:** `scripts/preprocess.py` (or merged into verify script as `--export` flag)

- Read `games.csv`
- Normalize: replace string `"nan"` and `""` with actual `NaN`
- Drop rows: `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `> 2010`
- Drop rows with any null in any column
- Convert `User_Score` to float, `Critic_Score` to int
- Output: `public/games_clean.json`

No backend needed — static JSON loaded at runtime.

---

## Phase 3 — Project Scaffold

```
games-dashboard/         (Vite + React)
  public/
    games_clean.json
  src/
    App.jsx              # two-tab layout
    components/
      FilterPanel.jsx    # platform multi-select, genre multi-select, year range
      KpiCards.jsx       # 3 KPI numbers
      charts/
        GenreBarChart.jsx
        PlatformPieChart.jsx
        YearTrendLine.jsx
      LLMSession.jsx     # Tab 2 — session log
    hooks/
      useFilteredGames.js  # load + filter logic
    styles/
      index.css
```

**Dependencies:** `recharts`, `react-select` (multi-select dropdowns)

---

## Phase 4 — Tab 1: Dashboard

### Filters (all applied simultaneously)
- **Platform** — multi-select (react-select)
- **Genre** — multi-select (react-select)
- **Year range** — two `<input type="range">` sliders for min/max year

### KPI Cards
1. Total Games (count)
2. Avg Critic Score
3. Avg User Score

### Charts (all react to filter changes)
1. **Games by Genre** — horizontal `BarChart` (Recharts)
2. **Platform Distribution** — `PieChart` / donut (Recharts)
3. **Releases Over Time** — `LineChart` by year (Recharts)

### Style
- Clean modern card layout, slate/indigo color palette
- CSS Grid: 2-col desktop → 1-col mobile (responsive)

---

## Phase 5 — Tab 2: LLM Session

- Export this Claude Code session as structured markdown (`llm-session.md`)
- Place in `public/llm-session.md`
- `LLMSession.jsx` fetches and renders it (using a markdown renderer like `react-markdown`)

---

## Phase 6 — Deployment

1. `npm run build` → `dist/`
2. Deploy to Vercel (`vercel --prod` or GitHub → Vercel dashboard)
3. Confirm public URL opens without login

---

## Verification Steps

1. Run `python scripts/verify_data.py` → review report, paste into LLM session log
2. Run preprocess → confirm row count delta matches verify report
3. Spot-check 2–3 KPI values manually against clean JSON
4. Verify all 3 charts update when filters change
5. Test responsive layout at 375px mobile width
6. Confirm Vercel URL is publicly accessible

input.content:
import pandas as pd
import sys

CSV_PATH = "games.csv"

def sep(title):
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"  {title}")
    print('='*50)

def main():
    df = pd.read_csv(CSV_PATH)

    sep("A. SCHEMA")
    expected = {"Name", "Platform", "Year_of_Release", "Genre", "Critic_Score", "User_Score", "Rating"}
    actual = set(df.columns)
    missing_cols = expected - actual
    extra_cols = actual - expected
    print(f"  Columns found    : {list(df.columns)}")
    print(f"  Total rows (raw) : {len(df)}")
    if missing_cols:
        print(f"  MISSING columns  : {missing_cols}")
    if extra_cols:
        print(f"  EXTRA columns    : {extra_cols}")
    if not missing_cols and not extra_cols:
        print("  Schema OK")

    sep("B. NULL / MISSING DETECTION")
    # Normalize: treat string "nan", "N/A", "" as missing
    df_norm = df.copy()
    for col in df_norm.select_dtypes(include="object").columns:
        df_norm[col] = df_norm[col].replace({"nan": None, "N/A": None, "": None})

    null_counts = df_norm.isnull().sum()
    for col, cnt in null_counts.items():
        flag = "  !! MISSING" if cnt > 0 else "  OK"
        print(f"  {col:<20}: {cnt:>5} nulls{flag}")

    sep("C. YEAR RANGE")
    years = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    print(f"  Min year : {years.min()}")
    print(f"  Max year : {years.max()}")
    out_of_range = ((years < 1990) | (years > 2010)).sum()
    non_numeric_year = years.isna().sum()
    print(f"  Outside [1990, 2010] : {out_of_range} rows")
    print(f"  Non-numeric year     : {non_numeric_year} rows")

    sep("D. SCORE RANGES")
    # Critic_Score
    critic = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    critic_non_num = df_norm["Critic_Score"].notna() & critic.isna()
    print(f"  Critic_Score  — non-numeric: {critic_non_num.sum()}, "
          f"out of range [0-100]: {((critic < 0) | (critic > 100)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {critic.isna().sum()}")

    # User_Score
    user = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["User_Score"], errors="coerce")
    user_non_num = df_norm["User_Score"].notna() & user.isna()
    print(f"  User_Score    — non-numeric: {user_non_num.sum()}, "
          f"out of range [0-10]: {((user < 0) | (user > 10)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {user.isna().sum()}")

    sep("E. RATING VALIDITY")
    allowed_ratings = {"E", "E10+", "T", "M", "AO", "K-A", "RP"}
    rating_counts = df_norm["Rating"].value_counts(dropna=False)
    print(f"  Value counts:")
    for val, cnt in rating_counts.items():
        flag = "  << UNEXPECTED" if pd.notna(val) and val not in allowed_ratings else ""
        print(f"    {str(val):<10}: {cnt}{flag}")

    sep("F. DUPLICATES")
    exact_dups = df.duplicated().sum()
    name_platform_dups = df.duplicated(subset=["Name", "Platform"]).sum()
    print(f"  Exact duplicate rows          : {exact_dups}")
    print(f"  Duplicate (Name, Platform)    : {name_platform_dups}")

    sep("G. POST-PREPROCESSING SUMMARY")
    df2 = df_norm.copy()
    df2["Year_of_Release"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    df2["Critic_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    df2["User_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["User_Score"], errors="coerce")

    before = len(df2)
    df2 = df2[(df2["Year_of_Release"] >= 1990) & (df2["Year_of_Release"] <= 2010)]
    after_year = len(df2)
    df2 = df2.dropna()
    after_null = len(df2)

    print(f"  Rows before          : {before}")
    print(f"  After year filter    : {after_year}  (removed {before - after_year})")
    print(f"  After drop nulls     : {after_null}  (removed {after_year - after_null})")
    print(f"  Total removed        : {before - after_null}")
    print(f"  Remaining nulls      : {df2.isnull().sum().sum()}")
    print(f"  Year range check     : {int(df2['Year_of_Release'].min())}–{int(df2['Year_of_Release'].max())}")
    print(f"  Unique platforms     : {df2['Platform'].nunique()}  {sorted(df2['Platform'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique genres        : {df2['Genre'].nunique()}  {sorted(df2['Genre'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique years         : {df2['Year_of_Release'].nunique()}")
    print(f"  Unique ratings       : {sorted(df2['Rating'].unique())}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

content.text:
Here's what the data tells us:

**Key findings:**

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Raw rows | 16,719 |
| After preprocessing | **5,115** (drops 69% of raw data) |
| `Critic_Score` nulls | 8,582 — the biggest driver of row loss |
| `User_Score` nulls | 6,704 + 2,425 non-numeric (string `"tbd"` likely) |
| `Rating` nulls | 6,769 |
| Year range outliers | 4,231 rows (1980–2020 in raw data) |
| Unexpected rating | `EC` (8 rows) — "Early Childhood", not in our allowed list |
| Duplicates | 2 exact, 5 `(Name, Platform)` pairs |

**Things to decide before preprocessing:**
- `User_Score` has 2,425 non-numeric values — likely `"tbd"` strings. These will be dropped as nulls, which is correct.
- `EC` rating (8 rows) — will be kept after preprocessing since it has no nulls. Should we treat it as valid or drop?
- Earliest clean year is **1992** (not 1990) — the filter says ≥1990 but no games survive to 1990/1991 after dropping nulls.

Ready to write `scripts/preprocess.py` next?

- User_Score has "tbd" strings (2,425 non-numeric values) → treat as null

input.content:
import pandas as pd
import json
import os

CSV_PATH = "games.csv"
OUT_PATH = os.path.join("public", "games_clean.json")

def main():
    df = pd.read_csv(CSV_PATH)
    print(f"Loaded: {len(df)} rows")

    # Normalize string "nan", "tbd", "" to actual NaN
    for col in df.columns:
        if df[col].dtype == object:
            df[col] = df[col].replace({"nan": None, "tbd": None, "N/A": None, "": None})

    # Parse numeric columns
    df["Year_of_Release"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    df["Critic_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    df["User_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df["User_Score"], errors="coerce")

    # Filter year range
    df = df[(df["Year_of_Release"] >= 1990) & (df["Year_of_Release"] <= 2010)]
    print(f"After year filter [1990–2010]: {len(df)} rows")

    # Drop any row with nulls in any column
    df = df.dropna()
    print(f"After dropping nulls: {len(df)} rows")

    # Cast types
    df["Year_of_Release"] = df["Year_of_Release"].astype(int)
    df["Critic_Score"] = df["Critic_Score"].astype(int)
    df["User_Score"] = df["User_Score"].round(1)

    # Sanity checks
    assert df.isnull().sum().sum() == 0, "Nulls remain after cleaning"
    assert df["Year_of_Release"].between(1990, 2010).all(), "Year out of range"

    print(f"\nClean dataset summary:")
    print(f"  Rows            : {len(df)}")
    print(f"  Platforms       : {sorted(df['Platform'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Genres          : {sorted(df['Genre'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Ratings         : {sorted(df['Rating'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Year range      : {df['Year_of_Release'].min()}–{df['Year_of_Release'].max()}")
    print(f"  Critic_Score    : {df['Critic_Score'].min()}–{df['Critic_Score'].max()}, mean={df['Critic_Score'].mean():.1f}")
    print(f"  User_Score      : {df['User_Score'].min()}–{df['User_Score'].max()}, mean={df['User_Score'].mean():.1f}")

    # Write output
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUT_PATH), exist_ok=True)
    records = df.to_dict(orient="records")
    with open(OUT_PATH, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(records, f, ensure_ascii=False)

    size_kb = os.path.getsize(OUT_PATH) / 1024
    print(f"\nSaved: {OUT_PATH} ({size_kb:.1f} KB)")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

input.content:
import pandas as pd
import json
import os

CSV_PATH = "games.csv"
OUT_JSON = os.path.join("public", "games_clean.json")
OUT_CLEAN_CSV = "games_clean.csv"
OUT_EXCLUDED_CSV = "games_excluded.csv"


def main():
    df = pd.read_csv(CSV_PATH)
    print(f"Loaded: {len(df)} rows")

    # Track excluded rows with reason
    excluded_parts = []

    # Normalize string "nan", "tbd", "" to actual NaN
    for col in df.columns:
        if df[col].dtype == object:
            df[col] = df[col].replace({"nan": None, "tbd": None, "N/A": None, "": None})

    # Parse numeric columns
    df["Year_of_Release"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    df["Critic_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    df["User_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df["User_Score"], errors="coerce")

    # --- Filter: year range ---
    out_of_year = df[~df["Year_of_Release"].between(1990, 2010)].copy()
    out_of_year["_exclude_reason"] = "year_out_of_range"
    excluded_parts.append(out_of_year)
    df = df[df["Year_of_Release"].between(1990, 2010)]
    print(f"After year filter [1990–2010]: {len(df)} rows  (excluded {len(out_of_year)})")

    # --- Filter: nulls ---
    has_null = df[df.isnull().any(axis=1)].copy()
    has_null["_exclude_reason"] = "missing_values"
    excluded_parts.append(has_null)
    df = df.dropna()
    print(f"After dropping nulls: {len(df)} rows  (excluded {len(has_null)})")

    # --- Filter: duplicate (Name, Platform) ---
    dup_mask = df.duplicated(subset=["Name", "Platform"], keep=False)
    dups = df[dup_mask].copy()

    # For each (Name, Platform) group: keep first only if ALL rows are identical; else drop all
    keep_idx = set()
    drop_idx = set()
    for (name, platform), group in dups.groupby(["Name", "Platform"]):
        cols = [c for c in group.columns]
        if group[cols].duplicated(keep=False).all():
            # All rows identical — keep first
            keep_idx.add(group.index[0])
            drop_idx.update(group.index[1:])
        else:
            # Rows differ — drop all
            drop_idx.update(group.index)

    ambiguous_dups = df.loc[list(drop_idx)].copy()
    ambiguous_dups["_exclude_reason"] = "duplicate_name_platform_conflict"
    excluded_parts.append(ambiguous_dups)
    df = df.drop(index=list(drop_idx))
    print(f"After dedup (Name, Platform): {len(df)} rows  (excluded {len(ambiguous_dups)})")

    # --- Cast types ---
    df["Year_of_Release"] = df["Year_of_Release"].astype(int)
    df["Critic_Score"] = df["Critic_Score"].astype(int)
    df["User_Score"] = df["User_Score"].round(1)
    df = df.reset_index(drop=True)

    # --- Assertions ---
    assert df.isnull().sum().sum() == 0, "Nulls remain after cleaning"
    assert df["Year_of_Release"].between(1990, 2010).all(), "Year out of range"
    assert not df.duplicated(subset=["Name", "Platform"]).any(), "Duplicate (Name, Platform) remain"

    print(f"\nClean dataset summary:")
    print(f"  Rows            : {len(df)}")
    print(f"  Platforms       : {sorted(df['Platform'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Genres          : {sorted(df['Genre'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Ratings         : {sorted(df['Rating'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Year range      : {df['Year_of_Release'].min()}–{df['Year_of_Release'].max()}")
    print(f"  Critic_Score    : {df['Critic_Score'].min()}–{df['Critic_Score'].max()}, mean={df['Critic_Score'].mean():.1f}")
    print(f"  User_Score      : {df['User_Score'].min()}–{df['User_Score'].max()}, mean={df['User_Score'].mean():.1f}")

    # --- Save games_clean.json ---
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUT_JSON), exist_ok=True)
    records = df.to_dict(orient="records")
    with open(OUT_JSON, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
        json.dump(records, f, ensure_ascii=False)
    print(f"\nSaved: {OUT_JSON} ({os.path.getsize(OUT_JSON)/1024:.1f} KB)")

    # --- Save games_clean.csv ---
    df.to_csv(OUT_CLEAN_CSV, index=False)
    print(f"Saved: {OUT_CLEAN_CSV}")

    # --- Save games_excluded.csv ---
    excluded = pd.concat(excluded_parts, ignore_index=True)
    # Restore original dtypes for excluded (Year may be NaN so keep as float)
    excluded.to_csv(OUT_EXCLUDED_CSV, index=False)
    print(f"Saved: {OUT_EXCLUDED_CSV} ({len(excluded)} rows excluded total)")
    print(f"\nExclusion breakdown:")
    print(excluded["_exclude_reason"].value_counts().to_string())


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import pandas as pd
import sys

CSV_PATH = "games.csv"

def sep(title):
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"  {title}")
    print('='*50)

def main():
    df = pd.read_csv(CSV_PATH)

    sep("A. SCHEMA")
    expected = {"Name", "Platform", "Year_of_Release", "Genre", "Critic_Score", "User_Score", "Rating"}
    actual = set(df.columns)
    missing_cols = expected - actual
    extra_cols = actual - expected
    print(f"  Columns found    : {list(df.columns)}")
    print(f"  Total rows (raw) : {len(df)}")
    if missing_cols:
        print(f"  MISSING columns  : {missing_cols}")
    if extra_cols:
        print(f"  EXTRA columns    : {extra_cols}")
    if not missing_cols and not extra_cols:
        print("  Schema OK")

    sep("B. NULL / MISSING DETECTION")
    # Normalize: treat string "nan", "N/A", "" as missing
    df_norm = df.copy()
    for col in df_norm.select_dtypes(include="object").columns:
        df_norm[col] = df_norm[col].replace({"nan": None, "N/A": None, "": None})

    null_counts = df_norm.isnull().sum()
    for col, cnt in null_counts.items():
        flag = "  !! MISSING" if cnt > 0 else "  OK"
        print(f"  {col:<20}: {cnt:>5} nulls{flag}")

    sep("C. YEAR RANGE")
    years = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    print(f"  Min year : {years.min()}")
    print(f"  Max year : {years.max()}")
    out_of_range = ((years < 1990) | (years > 2010)).sum()
    non_numeric_year = years.isna().sum()
    print(f"  Outside [1990, 2010] : {out_of_range} rows")
    print(f"  Non-numeric year     : {non_numeric_year} rows")

    sep("D. SCORE RANGES")
    # Critic_Score
    critic = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    critic_non_num = df_norm["Critic_Score"].notna() & critic.isna()
    print(f"  Critic_Score  — non-numeric: {critic_non_num.sum()}, "
          f"out of range [0-100]: {((critic < 0) | (critic > 100)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {critic.isna().sum()}")

    # User_Score
    user = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["User_Score"], errors="coerce")
    user_non_num = df_norm["User_Score"].notna() & user.isna()
    print(f"  User_Score    — non-numeric: {user_non_num.sum()}, "
          f"out of range [0-10]: {((user < 0) | (user > 10)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {user.isna().sum()}")

    sep("E. RATING VALIDITY")
    allowed_ratings = {"E", "E10+", "T", "M", "AO", "K-A", "RP"}
    rating_counts = df_norm["Rating"].value_counts(dropna=False)
    print(f"  Value counts:")
    for val, cnt in rating_counts.items():
        flag = "  << UNEXPECTED" if pd.notna(val) and val not in allowed_ratings else ""
        print(f"    {str(val):<10}: {cnt}{flag}")

    sep("F. DUPLICATES")
    exact_dups = df.duplicated().sum()
    name_platform_dups = df.duplicated(subset=["Name", "Platform"]).sum()
    print(f"  Exact duplicate rows          : {exact_dups}")
    print(f"  Duplicate (Name, Platform)    : {name_platform_dups}")

    sep("G. POST-PREPROCESSING SUMMARY")
    df2 = df_norm.copy()
    df2["Year_of_Release"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    df2["Critic_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    df2["User_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["User_Score"], errors="coerce")

    before = len(df2)
    df2 = df2[(df2["Year_of_Release"] >= 1990) & (df2["Year_of_Release"] <= 2010)]
    after_year = len(df2)
    df2 = df2.dropna()
    after_null = len(df2)

    print(f"  Rows before          : {before}")
    print(f"  After year filter    : {after_year}  (removed {before - after_year})")
    print(f"  After drop nulls     : {after_null}  (removed {after_year - after_null})")
    print(f"  Total removed        : {before - after_null}")
    print(f"  Remaining nulls      : {df2.isnull().sum().sum()}")
    print(f"  Year range check     : {int(df2['Year_of_Release'].min())}–{int(df2['Year_of_Release'].max())}")
    print(f"  Unique platforms     : {df2['Platform'].nunique()}  {sorted(df2['Platform'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique genres        : {df2['Genre'].nunique()}  {sorted(df2['Genre'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique years         : {df2['Year_of_Release'].nunique()}")
    print(f"  Unique ratings       : {sorted(df2['Rating'].unique())}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

file.content:
import pandas as pd
import sys

CSV_PATH = "games.csv"

def sep(title):
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"  {title}")
    print('='*50)

def main():
    df = pd.read_csv(CSV_PATH)

    sep("A. SCHEMA")
    expected = {"Name", "Platform", "Year_of_Release", "Genre", "Critic_Score", "User_Score", "Rating"}
    actual = set(df.columns)
    missing_cols = expected - actual
    extra_cols = actual - expected
    print(f"  Columns found    : {list(df.columns)}")
    print(f"  Total rows (raw) : {len(df)}")
    if missing_cols:
        print(f"  MISSING columns  : {missing_cols}")
    if extra_cols:
        print(f"  EXTRA columns    : {extra_cols}")
    if not missing_cols and not extra_cols:
        print("  Schema OK")

    sep("B. NULL / MISSING DETECTION")
    # Normalize: treat string "nan", "N/A", "" as missing
    df_norm = df.copy()
    for col in df_norm.select_dtypes(include="object").columns:
        df_norm[col] = df_norm[col].replace({"nan": None, "N/A": None, "": None})

    null_counts = df_norm.isnull().sum()
    for col, cnt in null_counts.items():
        flag = "  !! MISSING" if cnt > 0 else "  OK"
        print(f"  {col:<20}: {cnt:>5} nulls{flag}")

    sep("C. YEAR RANGE")
    years = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    print(f"  Min year : {years.min()}")
    print(f"  Max year : {years.max()}")
    out_of_range = ((years < 1990) | (years > 2010)).sum()
    non_numeric_year = years.isna().sum()
    print(f"  Outside [1990, 2010] : {out_of_range} rows")
    print(f"  Non-numeric year     : {non_numeric_year} rows")

    sep("D. SCORE RANGES")
    # Critic_Score
    critic = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    critic_non_num = df_norm["Critic_Score"].notna() & critic.isna()
    print(f"  Critic_Score  — non-numeric: {critic_non_num.sum()}, "
          f"out of range [0-100]: {((critic < 0) | (critic > 100)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {critic.isna().sum()}")

    # User_Score
    user = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["User_Score"], errors="coerce")
    user_non_num = df_norm["User_Score"].notna() & user.isna()
    print(f"  User_Score    — non-numeric: {user_non_num.sum()}, "
          f"out of range [0-10]: {((user < 0) | (user > 10)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {user.isna().sum()}")

    sep("E. RATING CHECK")
    rating_null = df_norm["Rating"].isna().sum()
    rating_counts = df_norm["Rating"].value_counts(dropna=True)
    flag = "  !! MISSING" if rating_null > 0 else "  OK"
    print(f"  Null/empty : {rating_null}{flag}")
    print(f"  Value counts (non-null):")
    for val, cnt in rating_counts.items():
        print(f"    {str(val):<10}: {cnt}")

    sep("F. DUPLICATES")
    exact_dups = df.duplicated().sum()
    name_platform_dups = df.duplicated(subset=["Name", "Platform"]).sum()
    print(f"  Exact duplicate rows          : {exact_dups}")
    print(f"  Duplicate (Name, Platform)    : {name_platform_dups}")

    sep("G. POST-PREPROCESSING SUMMARY")
    df2 = df_norm.copy()
    df2["Year_of_Release"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    df2["Critic_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    df2["User_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["User_Score"], errors="coerce")

    before = len(df2)
    df2 = df2[(df2["Year_of_Release"] >= 1990) & (df2["Year_of_Release"] <= 2010)]
    after_year = len(df2)
    df2 = df2.dropna()
    after_null = len(df2)

    print(f"  Rows before          : {before}")
    print(f"  After year filter    : {after_year}  (removed {before - after_year})")
    print(f"  After drop nulls     : {after_null}  (removed {after_year - after_null})")
    print(f"  Total removed        : {before - after_null}")
    print(f"  Remaining nulls      : {df2.isnull().sum().sum()}")
    print(f"  Year range check     : {int(df2['Year_of_Release'].min())}–{int(df2['Year_of_Release'].max())}")
    print(f"  Unique platforms     : {df2['Platform'].nunique()}  {sorted(df2['Platform'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique genres        : {df2['Genre'].nunique()}  {sorted(df2['Genre'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique years         : {df2['Year_of_Release'].nunique()}")
    print(f"  Unique ratings       : {sorted(df2['Rating'].unique())}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

    # User_Score
    user = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["User_Score"], errors="coerce")

          f"out of range [0-10]: {((user < 0) | (user > 10)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {user.isna().sum()}")

    sep("E. RATING CHECK")
    rating_null = df_norm["Rating"].isna().sum()

    sep("G. POST-PREPROCESSING SUMMARY")
    df2 = df_norm.copy()
    df2["Year_of_Release"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    df2["Critic_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")

    before = len(df2)

    print(f"  Unique years         : {df2['Year_of_Release'].nunique()}")
    print(f"  Unique ratings       : {sorted(df2['Rating'].unique())}")

toolUseResult.originalFile:
import pandas as pd
import sys

CSV_PATH = "games.csv"

def sep(title):
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"  {title}")
    print('='*50)

def main():
    df = pd.read_csv(CSV_PATH)

    sep("A. SCHEMA")
    expected = {"Name", "Platform", "Year_of_Release", "Genre", "Critic_Score", "User_Score", "Rating"}
    actual = set(df.columns)
    missing_cols = expected - actual
    extra_cols = actual - expected
    print(f"  Columns found    : {list(df.columns)}")
    print(f"  Total rows (raw) : {len(df)}")
    if missing_cols:
        print(f"  MISSING columns  : {missing_cols}")
    if extra_cols:
        print(f"  EXTRA columns    : {extra_cols}")
    if not missing_cols and not extra_cols:
        print("  Schema OK")

    sep("A1. RAW DATA SUMMARY")
    raw_years = pd.to_numeric(df["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    print(f"  Rows             : {len(df)}")
    print(f"  Year range       : {raw_years.min()}–{raw_years.max()}")
    print(f"  Unique platforms : {df['Platform'].nunique()}  {sorted(df['Platform'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique genres    : {df['Genre'].nunique()}  {sorted(df['Genre'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique years     : {raw_years.nunique()}")
    print(f"  Unique ratings   : {sorted(df['Rating'].unique())}")

    sep("B. NULL / MISSING DETECTION")
    # Normalize: treat string "nan", "N/A", "" as missing
    df_norm = df.copy()
    for col in df_norm.select_dtypes(include="object").columns:
        df_norm[col] = df_norm[col].replace({"nan": None, "N/A": None, "": None})

    null_counts = df_norm.isnull().sum()
    for col, cnt in null_counts.items():
        flag = "  !! MISSING" if cnt > 0 else "  OK"
        print(f"  {col:<20}: {cnt:>5} nulls{flag}")

    sep("C. YEAR RANGE")
    years = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    print(f"  Min year : {years.min()}")
    print(f"  Max year : {years.max()}")
    out_of_range = ((years < 1990) | (years > 2010)).sum()
    non_numeric_year = years.isna().sum()
    print(f"  Outside [1990, 2010] : {out_of_range} rows")
    print(f"  Non-numeric year     : {non_numeric_year} rows")

    sep("D. SCORE RANGES")
    # Critic_Score
    critic = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    critic_non_num = df_norm["Critic_Score"].notna() & critic.isna()
    print(f"  Critic_Score  — non-numeric: {critic_non_num.sum()}, "
          f"out of range [0-100]: {((critic < 0) | (critic > 100)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {critic.isna().sum()}")

    # User_Score
    user = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["User_Score"], errors="coerce")
    user_non_num = df_norm["User_Score"].notna() & user.isna()
    print(f"  User_Score    — non-numeric: {user_non_num.sum()}, "
          f"out of range [0-10]: {((user < 0) | (user > 10)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {user.isna().sum()}")

    sep("E. RATING CHECK")
    rating_null = df_norm["Rating"].isna().sum()
    rating_counts = df_norm["Rating"].value_counts(dropna=True)
    flag = "  !! MISSING" if rating_null > 0 else "  OK"
    print(f"  Null/empty : {rating_null}{flag}")
    print(f"  Value counts (non-null):")
    for val, cnt in rating_counts.items():
        print(f"    {str(val):<10}: {cnt}")

    sep("F. DUPLICATES")
    exact_dups = df.duplicated().sum()
    name_platform_dups = df.duplicated(subset=["Name", "Platform"]).sum()
    print(f"  Exact duplicate rows          : {exact_dups}")
    print(f"  Duplicate (Name, Platform)    : {name_platform_dups}")

    sep("G. POST-PREPROCESSING SUMMARY")
    df2 = df_norm.copy()
    df2["Year_of_Release"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    df2["Critic_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    df2["User_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["User_Score"], errors="coerce")

    before = len(df2)
    df2 = df2[(df2["Year_of_Release"] >= 1990) & (df2["Year_of_Release"] <= 2010)]
    after_year = len(df2)
    df2 = df2.dropna()
    after_null = len(df2)

    print(f"  Rows before          : {before}")
    print(f"  After year filter    : {after_year}  (removed {before - after_year})")
    print(f"  After drop nulls     : {after_null}  (removed {after_year - after_null})")
    print(f"  Total removed        : {before - after_null}")
    print(f"  Remaining nulls      : {df2.isnull().sum().sum()}")
    print(f"  Year range check     : {int(df2['Year_of_Release'].min())}–{int(df2['Year_of_Release'].max())}")
    print(f"  Unique platforms     : {df2['Platform'].nunique()}  {sorted(df2['Platform'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique genres        : {df2['Genre'].nunique()}  {sorted(df2['Genre'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique years         : {df2['Year_of_Release'].nunique()}")
    print(f"  Unique ratings       : {sorted(df2['Rating'].unique())}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

toolUseResult.originalFile:
import pandas as pd
import sys

CSV_PATH = "games.csv"

def sep(title):
    print(f"\n{'='*50}")
    print(f"  {title}")
    print('='*50)

def main():
    df = pd.read_csv(CSV_PATH)

    sep("A. SCHEMA")
    expected = {"Name", "Platform", "Year_of_Release", "Genre", "Critic_Score", "User_Score", "Rating"}
    actual = set(df.columns)
    missing_cols = expected - actual
    extra_cols = actual - expected
    print(f"  Columns found    : {list(df.columns)}")
    print(f"  Total rows (raw) : {len(df)}")
    if missing_cols:
        print(f"  MISSING columns  : {missing_cols}")
    if extra_cols:
        print(f"  EXTRA columns    : {extra_cols}")
    if not missing_cols and not extra_cols:
        print("  Schema OK")

    sep("A1. RAW DATA SUMMARY")
    raw_years = pd.to_numeric(df["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    srt = lambda vals: sorted(vals, key=lambda x: str(x))
    print(f"  Rows             : {len(df)}")
    print(f"  Year range       : {raw_years.min()}–{raw_years.max()}")
    print(f"  Unique platforms : {df['Platform'].nunique()}  {srt(df['Platform'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique genres    : {df['Genre'].nunique()}  {srt(df['Genre'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique years     : {raw_years.nunique()}")
    print(f"  Unique ratings   : {srt(df['Rating'].unique())}")

    sep("B. NULL / MISSING DETECTION")
    # Normalize: treat string "nan", "N/A", "" as missing
    df_norm = df.copy()
    for col in df_norm.select_dtypes(include="object").columns:
        df_norm[col] = df_norm[col].replace({"nan": None, "N/A": None, "": None})

    null_counts = df_norm.isnull().sum()
    for col, cnt in null_counts.items():
        flag = "  !! MISSING" if cnt > 0 else "  OK"
        print(f"  {col:<20}: {cnt:>5} nulls{flag}")

    sep("C. YEAR RANGE")
    years = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    print(f"  Min year : {years.min()}")
    print(f"  Max year : {years.max()}")
    out_of_range = ((years < 1990) | (years > 2010)).sum()
    non_numeric_year = years.isna().sum()
    print(f"  Outside [1990, 2010] : {out_of_range} rows")
    print(f"  Non-numeric year     : {non_numeric_year} rows")

    sep("D. SCORE RANGES")
    # Critic_Score
    critic = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    critic_non_num = df_norm["Critic_Score"].notna() & critic.isna()
    print(f"  Critic_Score  — non-numeric: {critic_non_num.sum()}, "
          f"out of range [0-100]: {((critic < 0) | (critic > 100)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {critic.isna().sum()}")

    # User_Score
    user = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["User_Score"], errors="coerce")
    user_non_num = df_norm["User_Score"].notna() & user.isna()
    print(f"  User_Score    — non-numeric: {user_non_num.sum()}, "
          f"out of range [0-10]: {((user < 0) | (user > 10)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {user.isna().sum()}")

    sep("E. RATING CHECK")
    rating_null = df_norm["Rating"].isna().sum()
    rating_counts = df_norm["Rating"].value_counts(dropna=True)
    flag = "  !! MISSING" if rating_null > 0 else "  OK"
    print(f"  Null/empty : {rating_null}{flag}")
    print(f"  Value counts (non-null):")
    for val, cnt in rating_counts.items():
        print(f"    {str(val):<10}: {cnt}")

    sep("F. DUPLICATES")
    exact_dups = df.duplicated().sum()
    name_platform_dups = df.duplicated(subset=["Name", "Platform"]).sum()
    print(f"  Exact duplicate rows          : {exact_dups}")
    print(f"  Duplicate (Name, Platform)    : {name_platform_dups}")

    sep("G. POST-PREPROCESSING SUMMARY")
    df2 = df_norm.copy()
    df2["Year_of_Release"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Year_of_Release"], errors="coerce")
    df2["Critic_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["Critic_Score"], errors="coerce")
    df2["User_Score"] = pd.to_numeric(df2["User_Score"], errors="coerce")

    before = len(df2)
    df2 = df2[(df2["Year_of_Release"] >= 1990) & (df2["Year_of_Release"] <= 2010)]
    after_year = len(df2)
    df2 = df2.dropna()
    after_null = len(df2)

    print(f"  Rows before          : {before}")
    print(f"  After year filter    : {after_year}  (removed {before - after_year})")
    print(f"  After drop nulls     : {after_null}  (removed {after_year - after_null})")
    print(f"  Total removed        : {before - after_null}")
    print(f"  Remaining nulls      : {df2.isnull().sum().sum()}")
    print(f"  Year range check     : {int(df2['Year_of_Release'].min())}–{int(df2['Year_of_Release'].max())}")
    print(f"  Unique platforms     : {df2['Platform'].nunique()}  {sorted(df2['Platform'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique genres        : {df2['Genre'].nunique()}  {sorted(df2['Genre'].unique())}")
    print(f"  Unique years         : {df2['Year_of_Release'].nunique()}")
    print(f"  Unique ratings       : {sorted(df2['Rating'].unique())}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

def sep(title):

def main():

    sep("A. SCHEMA")
    expected = {"Name", "Platform", "Year_of_Release", "Genre", "Critic_Score", "User_Score", "Rating"}

    extra_cols = actual - expected
    print(f"  Columns found    : {list(df.columns)}")
    print(f"  Total rows (raw) : {len(df)}")
    if missing_cols:
        print(f"  MISSING columns  : {missing_cols}")
    if extra_cols:

    raw_year_set = set(raw_years.dropna().astype(int))

    print(f"  Unique years     : {raw_years.nunique()}")

    print(f"  Unique ratings   : {srt(df['Rating'].unique())}")

    sep("B. NULL / MISSING DETECTION")
    # Normalize: treat string "nan", "N/A", "" as missing
    df_norm = df.copy()

        df_norm[col] = df_norm[col].replace({"nan": None, "N/A": None, "": None})

    null_counts = df_norm.isnull().sum()

          f"out of range [0-100]: {((critic < 0) | (critic > 100)).sum()}, "
          f"null: {critic.isna().sum()}")

    # User_Score
    user = pd.to_numeric(df_norm["User_Score"], errors="coerce")

## Phase 1 — Data Quality Verification (Python/pandas)

**File:** `scripts/verify_data.py`  
Run once before building; outputs a compact text report — Claude reads the report, not the raw CSV.

### Verification Rules

**A. Schema**
- All 7 columns present: `Name`, `Platform`, `Year_of_Release`, `Genre`, `Critic_Score`, `User_Score`, `Rating`

**B. Null / missing detection**
- Null count per column (includes actual `NaN`, string `"nan"`, empty string `""`)
- Flag any column with missing > 0

**C. Year range**
- Count rows where `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `> 2010`
- Show min/max year in dataset

**D. Score ranges**
- `Critic_Score`: must be numeric, 0–100
- `User_Score`: must be numeric, 0–10
- Report non-numeric values and out-of-range counts separately

**E. Rating validity**
- Allowed values: `E`, `E10+`, `T`, `M`, `AO`, `K-A`, `RP`
- Report any unexpected values

**F. Duplicates**
- Exact duplicate rows
- Duplicate `(Name, Platform)` pairs (same game on same platform)

**G. Post-preprocessing summary**
- Apply the two required filters (year range + drop any-null rows)
- Print: rows before → rows removed → rows after
- Confirm: 0 nulls remain, all years in [1990, 2010]
- Print unique counts: platforms, genres, years

### Output
Script prints a structured report to stdout (easily pasteable into LLM session log).

---

## Phase 2 — Data Preprocessing

**File:** `scripts/preprocess.py` (or merged into verify script as `--export` flag)

- Read `games.csv`
- Normalize: replace string `"nan"` and `""` with actual `NaN`
- Drop rows: `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `> 2010`
- Drop rows with any null in any column
- Convert `User_Score` to float, `Critic_Score` to int
- Output: `public/games_clean.json`

No backend needed — static JSON loaded at runtime.
input.new_string:
## Phase 1 — Data Quality Verification ✅ DONE

**File:** `scripts/verify_data.py`  
Run before building. Prints a structured report to stdout — Claude reads the report, not the raw CSV (token economy).

### Sections

**A. Schema** — confirms all 7 expected columns are present.

**A1. Raw Data Summary** — snapshot of the raw CSV as-is (no filtering):
- Total rows, year range (min–max)
- Unique platforms, genres, ratings (including `nan` values, sorted safely)
- Unique year count + list of empty years within the raw range

**B. Null / Missing Detection** — null count per column (normalizes string `"nan"`, `"N/A"`, `""` to actual NaN before counting). Flags any column with nulls.

**C. Year Range** — min/max year, count of rows outside `[1990, 2010]`, count of non-numeric year values.

**D. Score Ranges** — for `Critic_Score` (0–100) and `User_Score` (0–10): counts non-numeric values, out-of-range values, and nulls separately. (`User_Score` has 2,425 `"tbd"` strings that become NaN.)

**E. Rating Check** — null count only (no allowlist); lists all distinct non-null values with counts.

**F. Duplicates** — exact duplicate rows; duplicate `(Name, Platform)` pairs.

**G. Post-Preprocessing Summary** — applies filters in sequence and reports counts:
- Rows before → after year filter → after drop nulls → total removed
- Confirms 0 nulls remain
- Unique platforms, genres, ratings, year count + list of empty years within clean range

### Key findings from run
- Raw: 16,719 rows, 31 platforms, years 1980–2020 (empty: 2018, 2019)
- Clean: 5,115 rows, 12 platforms, years 1992–2010 (empty: 1993, 1995)
- Biggest drop driver: `Critic_Score` (8,582 nulls) and `Rating` (6,769 nulls)

---

## Phase 2 — Data Preprocessing ✅ DONE

**File:** `scripts/preprocess.py`

Steps applied in order:
1. Normalize string `"nan"`, `"tbd"`, `"N/A"`, `""` → actual `NaN`
2. Parse `Year_of_Release`, `Critic_Score`, `User_Score` to numeric (non-parseable → NaN)
3. Drop rows where `Year_of_Release` outside `[1990, 2010]` — removes 4,500 rows
4. Drop rows with any null in any column — removes 7,104 rows
5. Deduplicate `(Name, Platform)`: keep first row only if all columns identical; drop all copies if any column differs — removed 0 rows (all dups were fully identical)
6. Cast: `Year_of_Release` → `int`, `Critic_Score` → `int`, `User_Score` → `float` (1 decimal)
7. Assert: 0 nulls, all years in `[1990, 2010]`, no duplicate `(Name, Platform)`

### Outputs
- `public/games_clean.json` — 5,115 records, 766 KB, loaded by the React app at runtime
- `games_clean.csv` — same data in CSV form for reference
- `games_excluded.csv` — 11,604 excluded rows with `_exclude_reason` column (`year_out_of_range` / `missing_values` / `duplicate_name_platform_conflict`)

# Games Dashboard — Implementation Plan (Task 3 only)

## Context
Build a production-grade two-tab interactive dashboard on `games.csv`, deployed to a public Vercel URL.
Stack: React + Recharts + Vite.

---

## Phase 1 — Data Quality Verification (Python/pandas)

**File:** `scripts/verify_data.py`  
Run once before building; outputs a compact text report — Claude reads the report, not the raw CSV.

### Verification Rules

**A. Schema**
- All 7 columns present: `Name`, `Platform`, `Year_of_Release`, `Genre`, `Critic_Score`, `User_Score`, `Rating`

**B. Null / missing detection**
- Null count per column (includes actual `NaN`, string `"nan"`, empty string `""`)
- Flag any column with missing > 0

**C. Year range**
- Count rows where `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `> 2010`
- Show min/max year in dataset

**D. Score ranges**
- `Critic_Score`: must be numeric, 0–100
- `User_Score`: must be numeric, 0–10
- Report non-numeric values and out-of-range counts separately

**E. Rating validity**
- Allowed values: `E`, `E10+`, `T`, `M`, `AO`, `K-A`, `RP`
- Report any unexpected values

**F. Duplicates**
- Exact duplicate rows
- Duplicate `(Name, Platform)` pairs (same game on same platform)

**G. Post-preprocessing summary**
- Apply the two required filters (year range + drop any-null rows)
- Print: rows before → rows removed → rows after
- Confirm: 0 nulls remain, all years in [1990, 2010]
- Print unique counts: platforms, genres, years

### Output
Script prints a structured report to stdout (easily pasteable into LLM session log).

---

## Phase 2 — Data Preprocessing

**File:** `scripts/preprocess.py` (or merged into verify script as `--export` flag)

- Read `games.csv`
- Normalize: replace string `"nan"` and `""` with actual `NaN`
- Drop rows: `Year_of_Release < 1990` or `> 2010`
- Drop rows with any null in any column
- Convert `User_Score` to float, `Critic_Score` to int
- Output: `public/games_clean.json`

No backend needed — static JSON loaded at runtime.

---

## Phase 3 — Project Scaffold

```
games-dashboard/         (Vite + React)
  public/
    games_clean.json
  src/
    App.jsx              # two-tab layout
    components/
      FilterPanel.jsx    # platform multi-select, genre multi-select, year range
      KpiCards.jsx       # 3 KPI numbers
      charts/
        GenreBarChart.jsx
        PlatformPieChart.jsx
        YearTrendLine.jsx
      LLMSession.jsx     # Tab 2 — session log
    hooks/
      useFilteredGames.js  # load + filter logic
    styles/
      index.css
```

**Dependencies:** `recharts`, `react-select` (multi-select dropdowns)

---

## Phase 4 — Tab 1: Dashboard

### Filters (all applied simultaneously)
- **Platform** — multi-select (react-select)
- **Genre** — multi-select (react-select)
- **Year range** — two `<input type="range">` sliders for min/max year

### KPI Cards
1. Total Games (count)
2. Avg Critic Score
3. Avg User Score

### Charts (all react to filter changes)
1. **Games by Genre** — horizontal `BarChart` (Recharts)
2. **Platform Distribution** — `PieChart` / donut (Recharts)
3. **Releases Over Time** — `LineChart` by year (Recharts)

### Style
- Clean modern card layout, slate/indigo color palette
- CSS Grid: 2-col desktop → 1-col mobile (responsive)

---

## Phase 5 — Tab 2: LLM Session

- Export this Claude Code session as structured markdown (`llm-session.md`)
- Place in `public/llm-session.md`
- `LLMSession.jsx` fetches and renders it (using a markdown renderer like `react-markdown`)

---

## Phase 6 — Deployment

1. `npm run build` → `dist/`
2. Deploy to Vercel (`vercel --prod` or GitHub → Vercel dashboard)
3. Confirm public URL opens without login

---

## Verification Steps

1. Run `python scripts/verify_data.py` → review report, paste into LLM session log
2. Run preprocess → confirm row count delta matches verify report
3. Spot-check 2–3 KPI values manually against clean JSON
4. Verify all 3 charts update when filters change
5. Test responsive layout at 375px mobile width
6. Confirm Vercel URL is publicly accessible

- **Genre** — multi-select (react-select)

2. **Platform Distribution** — `PieChart` / donut (Recharts)
3. **Releases Over Time** — `LineChart` by year (Recharts)

- CSS Grid: 2-col desktop → 1-col mobile (responsive)

# Games Dashboard — Implementation Plan (Task 3 only)

## Context
Build a production-grade two-tab interactive dashboard on `games.csv`, deployed to a public Vercel URL.
Stack: React + Recharts + Vite.

---

## Phase 1 — Data Quality Verification ✅ DONE

**File:** `scripts/verify_data.py`  
Run before building. Prints a structured report to stdout — Claude reads the report, not the raw CSV (token economy).

### Sections

**A. Schema** — confirms all 7 expected columns are present.

**A1. Raw Data Summary** — snapshot of the raw CSV as-is (no filtering):
- Total rows, year range (min–max)
- Unique platforms, genres, ratings (including `nan` values, sorted safely)
- Unique year count + list of empty years within the raw range

**B. Null / Missing Detection** — null count per column (normalizes string `"nan"`, `"N/A"`, `""` to actual NaN before counting). Flags any column with nulls.

**C. Year Range** — min/max year, count of rows outside `[1990, 2010]`, count of non-numeric year values.

**D. Score Ranges** — for `Critic_Score` (0–100) and `User_Score` (0–10): counts non-numeric values, out-of-range values, and nulls separately. (`User_Score` has 2,425 `"tbd"` strings that become NaN.)

**E. Rating Check** — null count only (no allowlist); lists all distinct non-null values with counts.

**F. Duplicates** — exact duplicate rows; duplicate `(Name, Platform)` pairs.

**G. Post-Preprocessing Summary** — applies filters in sequence and reports counts:
- Rows before → after year filter → after drop nulls → total removed
- Confirms 0 nulls remain
- Unique platforms, genres, ratings, year count + list of empty years within clean range

### Key findings from run
- Raw: 16,719 rows, 31 platforms, years 1980–2020 (empty: 2018, 2019)
- Clean: 5,115 rows, 12 platforms, years 1992–2010 (empty: 1993, 1995)
- Biggest drop driver: `Critic_Score` (8,582 nulls) and `Rating` (6,769 nulls)

---

## Phase 2 — Data Preprocessing ✅ DONE

**File:** `scripts/preprocess.py`

Steps applied in order:
1. Normalize string `"nan"`, `"tbd"`, `"N/A"`, `""` → actual `NaN`
2. Parse `Year_of_Release`, `Critic_Score`, `User_Score` to numeric (non-parseable → NaN)
3. Drop rows where `Year_of_Release` outside `[1990, 2010]` — removes 4,500 rows
4. Drop rows with any null in any column — removes 7,104 rows
5. Deduplicate `(Name, Platform)`: keep first row only if all columns identical; drop all copies if any column differs — removed 0 rows (all dups were fully identical)
6. Cast: `Year_of_Release` → `int`, `Critic_Score` → `int`, `User_Score` → `float` (1 decimal)
7. Assert: 0 nulls, all years in `[1990, 2010]`, no duplicate `(Name, Platform)`

### Outputs
- `public/games_clean.json` — 5,115 records, 766 KB, loaded by the React app at runtime
- `games_clean.csv` — same data in CSV form for reference
- `games_excluded.csv` — 11,604 excluded rows with `_exclude_reason` column (`year_out_of_range` / `missing_values` / `duplicate_name_platform_conflict`)

---

## Phase 3 — Project Scaffold

```
games-dashboard/         (Vite + React)
  public/
    games_clean.json
  src/
    App.jsx              # two-tab layout
    components/
      FilterPanel.jsx    # platform multi-select, genre multi-select, year range
      KpiCards.jsx       # 3 KPI numbers
      charts/
        GenreBarChart.jsx
        PlatformPieChart.jsx
        YearTrendLine.jsx
      LLMSession.jsx     # Tab 2 — session log
    hooks/
      useFilteredGames.js  # load + filter logic
    styles/
      index.css
```

**Dependencies:** `recharts`, `react-select` (multi-select dropdowns)

---

## Phase 4 — Tab 1: Dashboard

### Filters (all applied simultaneously)
- **Platform** — multi-select (react-select)
- **Genre** — multi-select (react-select)
- **Year range** — two `<input type="range">` sliders for min/max year

### KPI Cards
1. Total Games (count)
2. Avg Critic Score
3. Avg User Score

### Charts (all react to filter changes)
1. **Games by Genre** — horizontal `BarChart` (Recharts)
2. **Platform Distribution** — `PieChart` / donut (Recharts)
3. **Releases Over Time** — `LineChart` by year (Recharts)

### Style
- Reference: `STYLE_GUIDE.md`
- CSS Grid: 2-col desktop → 1-col mobile (responsive)

---

## Phase 5 — Tab 2: LLM Session

- Export this Claude Code session as structured markdown (`llm-session.md`)
- Place in `public/llm-session.md`
- `LLMSession.jsx` fetches and renders it (using `react-markdown`)

---

## Phase 6 — Deployment

1. `npm run build` → `dist/`
2. Deploy to Vercel (`vercel --prod` or GitHub → Vercel dashboard)
3. Confirm public URL opens without login

---

## Verification Steps

1. Run `python scripts/verify_data.py` → review report, paste into LLM session log
2. Run preprocess → confirm row count delta matches verify report
3. Spot-check 2–3 KPI values manually against clean JSON
4. Verify all 3 charts update when filters change
5. Test responsive layout at 375px mobile width
6. Confirm Vercel URL is publicly accessible
